{| class=“wikitable” style=“width: 98%; border: 2px solid gray;” |- {{Ägyptologie TblHighlight}} |colspan=“6” style=“text-align:center; background:#FFE39B”|’’‘Comparison of Solar and Sothis Year (Period 2770 to 1500 BC)’’’Jean Meeus: ‘‘Astronomical Algorithms: Applications for Ephemeris Tool 4, 5.’’ Barth, Leipzig 2000 for: ‘‘Ephemeris Tool 4,5’’ according to Jean Meeus: ‘‘Conversion Program, 2001’’. |- {{Ägyptologie TblHighlight}} ! {{ill|Observation site|de|Beobachtungsort|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Observation site|qq=|WD=Q184876}} ! {{ill|1 Sothis year|de|1 Sothisjahr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1 Sothis year|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}} ! {{ill|1 solar year|de|1 Sonnenjahr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1 solar year|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}} ! {{ill|Deviation|de|Abweichung|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Deviation|qq=|WD=Q3706278}} ! {{ill|Shift of one day after:|de|Verschiebung von einem Tag nach:|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shift of one day after:|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}} |- style=“background:#F5F5F5” |[[:en:Nile Delta|Nile Delta]] |style=“text-align:center” | 365.25001 days |style=“text-align:center” | 365.24208 daysFrom [[:en:Summer solstice|summer solstice]] to the next summer solstice, based on the period 2770 BC to 1500 BC. |style=“text-align:center” | 0.0079298 days |style=“text-align:center” | 126 years + 39 days |- style=“background:#FFFAF0” |[[:en:Elephantine|Elephantine]] |style=“text-align:center” | 365.25002 days |style=“text-align:center” | 365.24208 days |style=“text-align:center” | 0.0079417 days |style=“text-align:center” | 125 years + 11 months |- |}
== See also == • [[:en:Book of Nut|Book of Nut]] • [[:en:Sothic cycle|Sothis cycle]]
== Literature == • Rolf Krauss: ‘‘Sothis and Lunar Data: Studies on the Astronomical and Technical Chronology of Ancient Egypt.’’ Gerstenberg, Hildesheim 1985, {{Falsche ISBN|3-8067-8086-X}}. • [[:en:Christian Leitz|Christian Leitz]]: ‘‘Studies on Egyptian Astronomy.’’ Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 1991, ISBN 3-447-03157-3. • {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|qq=|WD=Q2643704}}: ‘‘Outline of the Course of the Stars: The so-called Book of Nut.’’ The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (among others), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5. • {{ill|Siegfried Schott|de|Siegfried Schott|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Siegfried Schott|qq=|WD=Q102228}}: ‘‘Ancient Egyptian Festal Dates.’’ Verlag der Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur, Mainz/Wiesbaden 1950.

{| class=“wikitable” style=“width: 98%; border: 2px solid gray;” |- {{Ägyptologie TblHighlight}} |colspan=“6” style=“text-align:center; background:#FFE39B”|’’‘Comparison of Solar and Sothis Year (Period 2770 to 1500 BC)’’’Jean Meeus: ‘‘Astronomical Algorithms: Applications for Ephemeris Tool 4, 5.’’ Barth, Leipzig 2000 for: ‘‘Ephemeris Tool 4,5’’ according to Jean Meeus: ‘‘Conversion Program, 2001’’. |- {{Ägyptologie TblHighlight}} ! {{ill|Observation site|de|Beobachtungsort|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Observation site|qq=|WD=Q184876}} ! {{ill|1 Sothis year|de|1 Sothisjahr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1 Sothis year|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}} ! {{ill|1 solar year|de|1 Sonnenjahr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1 solar year|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}} ! {{ill|Deviation|de|Abweichung|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Deviation|qq=|WD=Q3706278}} ! {{ill|Shift of one day after:|de|Verschiebung von einem Tag nach:|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shift of one day after:|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}} |- style=“background:#F5F5F5” |[[:en:Nile Delta|Nile Delta]] |style=“text-align:center” | 365.25001 days |style=“text-align:center” | 365.24208 daysFrom [[:en:Summer solstice|summer solstice]] to the next summer solstice, based on the period 2770 BC to 1500 BC. |style=“text-align:center” | 0.0079298 days |style=“text-align:center” | 126 years + 39 days |- style=“background:#FFFAF0” |[[:en:Elephantine|Elephantine]] |style=“text-align:center” | 365.25002 days |style=“text-align:center” | 365.24208 days |style=“text-align:center” | 0.0079417 days |style=“text-align:center” | 125 years + 11 months |- |}

The term ‘’‘Sothis year’’’ (also ‘’’[[:en:Sopdet|Sopdet]] year’’’) originally referred in [[:en:Ancient Egypt|Ancient Egypt]] to the {{ill|calendar period|de|Kalenderzeitraum|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/calendar period|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}} between two heliacal risings of the [[:en:Star|star]] [[:en:Sirius|Sirius]].
== Background == With the beginning of the {{ill|Middle Kingdom|de|Mittleren Reiches|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Middle Kingdom|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}, the duration of a Sothis year—in connection with the [[:en:Coffin Texts|Coffin Texts]] and {{ill|diagonal star clock|de|Diagonalsternuhr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/diagonal star clock|qq=|WD=Q142353}}en—was additionally applied to the periods between two {{ill|Sirius culminations|de|Sirius-Kulminationen|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sirius culminations|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}} or between two {{ill|akronychic Sirius settings|de|akronychischen Sirius-Untergängen|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/akronychic Sirius settings|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}.
Before the introduction of the Egyptian administrative calendar, to which the {{ill|Egyptian civil lunar calendar|de|ägyptische bürgerliche Mondkalender|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Egyptian civil lunar calendar|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}} was oriented, the Egyptians followed the Sothis year, which on average—at 365,250015 days—provided the original division of the Egyptian calendar year and roughly corresponded to the [[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]]. The {{ill|seasons|de|Jahreszeiten|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/seasons|qq=|WD=Q24384}} of the Sothis year shifted, compared to the actual {{ill|tropical year|de|Sonnenjahr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tropical year|qq=|WD=Q577}}, by approximately one day every 126 years.

The {{ill|seasons|de|Jahreszeiten|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/seasons|qq=|WD=Q24384}} of the Sothis year shifted, compared to the actual {{ill|tropical year|de|Sonnenjahr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tropical year|qq=|WD=Q577}}, by approximately one day every 126 years.
{| class=“wikitable” style=“width: 98%; border: 2px solid gray;” |- {{Ägyptologie TblHighlight}} |colspan=“6” style=“text-align:center; background:#FFE39B”|’’‘Comparison of Solar and Sothis Year (Period 2770 to 1500 BC)’’’Jean Meeus: ‘‘Astronomical Algorithms: Applications for Ephemeris Tool 4, 5.’’ Barth, Leipzig 2000 for: ‘‘Ephemeris Tool 4,5’’ according to Jean Meeus: ‘‘Conversion Program, 2001’’. |- {{Ägyptologie TblHighlight}} ! {{ill|Observation site|de|Beobachtungsort|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Observation site|qq=|WD=Q184876}} ! {{ill|1 Sothis year|de|1 Sothisjahr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1 Sothis year|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}} ! {{ill|1 solar year|de|1 Sonnenjahr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1 solar year|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}} ! {{ill|Deviation|de|Abweichung|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Deviation|qq=|WD=Q3706278}} ! {{ill|Shift of one day after:|de|Verschiebung von einem Tag nach:|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shift of one day after:|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}} |- style=“background:#F5F5F5” |[[:en:Nile Delta|Nile Delta]] |style=“text-align:center” | 365.25001 days |style=“text-align:center” | 365.24208 daysFrom [[:en:Summer solstice|summer solstice]] to the next summer solstice, based on the period 2770 BC to 1500 BC. |style=“text-align:center” | 0.0079298 days |style=“text-align:center” | 126 years + 39 days |- style=“background:#FFFAF0” |[[:en:Elephantine|Elephantine]] |style=“text-align:center” | 365.25002 days |style=“text-align:center” | 365.24208 days |style=“text-align:center” | 0.0079417 days |style=“text-align:center” | 125 years + 11 months |- |}
== See also == • [[:en:Book of Nut|Book of Nut]] • [[:en:Sothic cycle|Sothis cycle]]
== Literature == • Rolf Krauss: ‘‘Sothis and Lunar Data: Studies on the Astronomical and Technical Chronology of Ancient Egypt.’’ Gerstenberg, Hildesheim 1985, {{Falsche ISBN|3-8067-8086-X}}. • [[:en:Christian Leitz|Christian Leitz]]: ‘‘Studies on Egyptian Astronomy.’’ Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 1991, ISBN 3-447-03157-3. • {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|qq=|WD=Q2643704}}: ‘‘Outline of the Course of the Stars: The so-called Book of Nut.’’ The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (among others), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5. • {{ill|Siegfried Schott|de|Siegfried Schott|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Siegfried Schott|qq=|WD=Q102228}}: ‘‘Ancient Egyptian Festal Dates.’’ Verlag der Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur, Mainz/Wiesbaden 1950.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[de:Sothisjahr]] [[File:CompareTropicalYears.png|alt=Definitions for the tropical year|thumb]]
The term ‘’‘Sothis year’’’ (also ‘’’[[:en:Sopdet|Sopdet]] year’’’) originally referred in [[:en:Ancient Egypt|Ancient Egypt]] to the {{ill|calendar period|de|Kalenderzeitraum|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/calendar period|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}} between two heliacal risings of the [[:en:Star|star]] [[:en:Sirius|Sirius]].
== Background == With the beginning of the {{ill|Middle Kingdom|de|Mittleren Reiches|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Middle Kingdom|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}, the duration of a Sothis year—in connection with the [[:en:Coffin Texts|Coffin Texts]] and {{ill|diagonal star clock|de|Diagonalsternuhr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/diagonal star clock|qq=|WD=Q142353}}en—was additionally applied to the periods between two {{ill|Sirius culminations|de|Sirius-Kulminationen|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sirius culminations|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}} or between two {{ill|akronychic Sirius settings|de|akronychischen Sirius-Untergängen|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/akronychic Sirius settings|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}.
Before the introduction of the Egyptian administrative calendar, to which the {{ill|Egyptian civil lunar calendar|de|ägyptische bürgerliche Mondkalender|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Egyptian civil lunar calendar|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}} was oriented, the Egyptians followed the Sothis year, which on average—at 365,250015 days—provided the original division of the Egyptian calendar year and roughly corresponded to the [[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]]. The {{ill|seasons|de|Jahreszeiten|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/seasons|qq=|WD=Q24384}} of the Sothis year shifted, compared to the actual {{ill|tropical year|de|Sonnenjahr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tropical year|qq=|WD=Q577}}, by approximately one day every 126 years.
{| class=“wikitable” style=“width: 98%; border: 2px solid gray;” |- {{Ägyptologie TblHighlight}} |colspan=“6” style=“text-align:center; background:#FFE39B”|’’‘Comparison of Solar and Sothis Year (Period 2770 to 1500 BC)’’’Jean Meeus: ‘‘Astronomical Algorithms: Applications for Ephemeris Tool 4, 5.’’ Barth, Leipzig 2000 for: ‘‘Ephemeris Tool 4,5’’ according to Jean Meeus: ‘‘Conversion Program, 2001’’. |- {{Ägyptologie TblHighlight}} ! {{ill|Observation site|de|Beobachtungsort|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Observation site|qq=|WD=Q184876}} ! {{ill|1 Sothis year|de|1 Sothisjahr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1 Sothis year|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}} ! {{ill|1 solar year|de|1 Sonnenjahr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1 solar year|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}} ! {{ill|Deviation|de|Abweichung|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Deviation|qq=|WD=Q3706278}} ! {{ill|Shift of one day after:|de|Verschiebung von einem Tag nach:|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shift of one day after:|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}} |- style=“background:#F5F5F5” |[[:en:Nile Delta|Nile Delta]] |style=“text-align:center” | 365.25001 days |style=“text-align:center” | 365.24208 daysFrom [[:en:Summer solstice|summer solstice]] to the next summer solstice, based on the period 2770 BC to 1500 BC. |style=“text-align:center” | 0.0079298 days |style=“text-align:center” | 126 years + 39 days |- style=“background:#FFFAF0” |[[:en:Elephantine|Elephantine]] |style=“text-align:center” | 365.25002 days |style=“text-align:center” | 365.24208 days |style=“text-align:center” | 0.0079417 days |style=“text-align:center” | 125 years + 11 months |- |}
== See also == • [[:en:Book of Nut|Book of Nut]] • [[:en:Sothic cycle|Sothis cycle]]

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[de:Sothisjahr]] [[File:CompareTropicalYears.png|alt=Definitions for the tropical year|thumb]]
The term ‘’‘Sothis year’’’ (also ‘’’[[:en:Sopdet|Sopdet]] year’’’) originally referred in [[:en:Ancient Egypt|Ancient Egypt]] to the {{ill|calendar period|de|Kalenderzeitraum|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/calendar period|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}} between two heliacal risings of the [[:en:Star|star]] [[:en:Sirius|Sirius]].
== Background == With the beginning of the {{ill|Middle Kingdom|de|Mittleren Reiches|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Middle Kingdom|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}, the duration of a Sothis year—in connection with the [[:en:Coffin Texts|Coffin Texts]] and {{ill|diagonal star clock|de|Diagonalsternuhr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/diagonal star clock|qq=|WD=Q142353}}en—was additionally applied to the periods between two {{ill|Sirius culminations|de|Sirius-Kulminationen|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sirius culminations|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}} or between two {{ill|akronychic Sirius settings|de|akronychischen Sirius-Untergängen|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/akronychic Sirius settings|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}.
Before the introduction of the Egyptian administrative calendar, to which the {{ill|Egyptian civil lunar calendar|de|ägyptische bürgerliche Mondkalender|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Egyptian civil lunar calendar|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}} was oriented, the Egyptians followed the Sothis year, which on average—at 365,250015 days—provided the original division of the Egyptian calendar year and roughly corresponded to the [[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]]. The {{ill|seasons|de|Jahreszeiten|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/seasons|qq=|WD=Q24384}} of the Sothis year shifted, compared to the actual {{ill|tropical year|de|Sonnenjahr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tropical year|qq=|WD=Q577}}, by approximately one day every 126 years.
{| class=“wikitable” style=“width: 98%; border: 2px solid gray;” |- {{Ägyptologie TblHighlight}} |colspan=“6” style=“text-align:center; background:#FFE39B”|’’‘Comparison of Solar and Sothis Year (Period 2770 to 1500 BC)’’’Jean Meeus: ‘‘Astronomical Algorithms: Applications for Ephemeris Tool 4, 5.’’ Barth, Leipzig 2000 for: ‘‘Ephemeris Tool 4,5’’ according to Jean Meeus: ‘‘Conversion Program, 2001’’. |- {{Ägyptologie TblHighlight}} ! {{ill|Observation site|de|Beobachtungsort|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Observation site|qq=|WD=Q184876}} ! {{ill|1 Sothis year|de|1 Sothisjahr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1 Sothis year|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}} ! {{ill|1 solar year|de|1 Sonnenjahr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1 solar year|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}} ! {{ill|Deviation|de|Abweichung|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Deviation|qq=|WD=Q3706278}} ! {{ill|Shift of one day after:|de|Verschiebung von einem Tag nach:|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shift of one day after:|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}} |- style=“background:#F5F5F5” |[[:en:Nile Delta|Nile Delta]] |style=“text-align:center” | 365.25001 days |style=“text-align:center” | 365.24208 daysFrom [[:en:Summer solstice|summer solstice]] to the next summer solstice, based on the period 2770 BC to 1500 BC. |style=“text-align:center” | 0.0079298 days |style=“text-align:center” | 126 years + 39 days |- style=“background:#FFFAF0” |[[:en:Elephantine|Elephantine]] |style=“text-align:center” | 365.25002 days |style=“text-align:center” | 365.24208 days |style=“text-align:center” | 0.0079417 days |style=“text-align:center” | 125 years + 11 months |- |}
== See also == • [[:en:Book of Nut|Book of Nut]] • [[:en:Sothic cycle|Sothis cycle]]
== Literature == • Rolf Krauss: ‘‘Sothis and Lunar Data: Studies on the Astronomical and Technical Chronology of Ancient Egypt.’’ Gerstenberg, Hildesheim 1985, {{Falsche ISBN|3-8067-8086-X}}. • [[:en:Christian Leitz|Christian Leitz]]: ‘‘Studies on Egyptian Astronomy.’’ Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 1991, ISBN 3-447-03157-3. • {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|qq=|WD=Q2643704}}: ‘‘Outline of the Course of the Stars: The so-called Book of Nut.’’ The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (among others), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5. • {{ill|Siegfried Schott|de|Siegfried Schott|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Siegfried Schott|qq=|WD=Q102228}}: ‘‘Ancient Egyptian Festal Dates.’’ Verlag der Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur, Mainz/Wiesbaden 1950.
== References ==

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[de:Sothisjahr]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[de:Sothisjahr]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[de:Sothisjahr]] [[File:CompareTropicalYears.png|alt=Definitions for the tropical year|thumb]]
The term ‘’‘Sothis year’’’ (also ‘’’[[:en:Sopdet|Sopdet]] year’’’) originally referred in [[:en:Ancient Egypt|Ancient Egypt]] to the {{ill|calendar period|de|Kalenderzeitraum|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/calendar period|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}} between two heliacal risings of the [[:en:Star|star]] [[:en:Sirius|Sirius]].
== Background == With the beginning of the {{ill|Middle Kingdom|de|Mittleren Reiches|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Middle Kingdom|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}, the duration of a Sothis year—in connection with the [[:en:Coffin Texts|Coffin Texts]] and {{ill|diagonal star clock|de|Diagonalsternuhr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/diagonal star clock|qq=|WD=Q142353}}en—was additionally applied to the periods between two {{ill|Sirius culminations|de|Sirius-Kulminationen|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sirius culminations|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}} or between two {{ill|akronychic Sirius settings|de|akronychischen Sirius-Untergängen|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/akronychic Sirius settings|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}.
Before the introduction of the Egyptian administrative calendar, to which the {{ill|Egyptian civil lunar calendar|de|ägyptische bürgerliche Mondkalender|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Egyptian civil lunar calendar|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}} was oriented, the Egyptians followed the Sothis year, which on average—at 365,250015 days—provided the original division of the Egyptian calendar year and roughly corresponded to the [[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]]. The {{ill|seasons|de|Jahreszeiten|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/seasons|qq=|WD=Q24384}} of the Sothis year shifted, compared to the actual {{ill|tropical year|de|Sonnenjahr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tropical year|qq=|WD=Q577}}, by approximately one day every 126 years.
{| class=“wikitable” style=“width: 98%; border: 2px solid gray;” |- {{Ägyptologie TblHighlight}} |colspan=“6” style=“text-align:center; background:#FFE39B”|’’‘Comparison of Solar and Sothis Year (Period 2770 to 1500 BC)’’’Jean Meeus: ‘‘Astronomical Algorithms: Applications for Ephemeris Tool 4, 5.’’ Barth, Leipzig 2000 for: ‘‘Ephemeris Tool 4,5’’ according to Jean Meeus: ‘‘Conversion Program, 2001’’. |- {{Ägyptologie TblHighlight}} ! {{ill|Observation site|de|Beobachtungsort|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Observation site|qq=|WD=Q184876}} ! {{ill|1 Sothis year|de|1 Sothisjahr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1 Sothis year|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}} ! {{ill|1 solar year|de|1 Sonnenjahr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1 solar year|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}} ! {{ill|Deviation|de|Abweichung|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Deviation|qq=|WD=Q3706278}} ! {{ill|Shift of one day after:|de|Verschiebung von einem Tag nach:|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shift of one day after:|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}} |- style=“background:#F5F5F5” |[[:en:Nile Delta|Nile Delta]] |style=“text-align:center” | 365.25001 days |style=“text-align:center” | 365.24208 daysFrom [[:en:Summer solstice|summer solstice]] to the next summer solstice, based on the period 2770 BC to 1500 BC. |style=“text-align:center” | 0.0079298 days |style=“text-align:center” | 126 years + 39 days |- style=“background:#FFFAF0” |[[:en:Elephantine|Elephantine]] |style=“text-align:center” | 365.25002 days |style=“text-align:center” | 365.24208 days |style=“text-align:center” | 0.0079417 days |style=“text-align:center” | 125 years + 11 months |- |}
== See also == • [[:en:Book of Nut|Book of Nut]] • [[:en:Sothic cycle|Sothis cycle]]
== Literature == • Rolf Krauss: ‘‘Sothis and Lunar Data: Studies on the Astronomical and Technical Chronology of Ancient Egypt.’’ Gerstenberg, Hildesheim 1985, {{Falsche ISBN|3-8067-8086-X}}. • [[:en:Christian Leitz|Christian Leitz]]: ‘‘Studies on Egyptian Astronomy.’’ Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 1991, ISBN 3-447-03157-3. • {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|qq=|WD=Q2643704}}: ‘‘Outline of the Course of the Stars: The so-called Book of Nut.’’ The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (among others), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5. • {{ill|Siegfried Schott|de|Siegfried Schott|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Siegfried Schott|qq=|WD=Q102228}}: ‘‘Ancient Egyptian Festal Dates.’’ Verlag der Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur, Mainz/Wiesbaden 1950.
== References ==

{{draft categories|
}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[de:Sothisjahr]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[de:Sothisjahr]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[de:Sothisjahr]] [[File:CompareTropicalYears.png|alt=Definitions for the tropical year|thumb]]
The term ‘’‘Sothis year’’’ (also ‘’’[[:en:Sopdet|Sopdet]] year’’’) originally referred in [[:en:Ancient Egypt|Ancient Egypt]] to the {{ill|calendar period|de|Kalenderzeitraum|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/calendar period|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}} between two heliacal risings of the [[:en:Star|star]] [[:en:Sirius|Sirius]].
== Background == With the beginning of the {{ill|Middle Kingdom|de|Mittleren Reiches|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Middle Kingdom|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}, the duration of a Sothis year—in connection with the [[:en:Coffin Texts|Coffin Texts]] and {{ill|diagonal star clock|de|Diagonalsternuhr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/diagonal star clock|qq=|WD=Q142353}}en—was additionally applied to the periods between two {{ill|Sirius culminations|de|Sirius-Kulminationen|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sirius culminations|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}} or between two {{ill|akronychic Sirius settings|de|akronychischen Sirius-Untergängen|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/akronychic Sirius settings|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}.
Before the introduction of the Egyptian administrative calendar, to which the {{ill|Egyptian civil lunar calendar|de|ägyptische bürgerliche Mondkalender|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Egyptian civil lunar calendar|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}} was oriented, the Egyptians followed the Sothis year, which on average—at 365,250015 days—provided the original division of the Egyptian calendar year and roughly corresponded to the [[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]]. The {{ill|seasons|de|Jahreszeiten|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/seasons|qq=|WD=Q24384}} of the Sothis year shifted, compared to the actual {{ill|tropical year|de|Sonnenjahr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tropical year|qq=|WD=Q577}}, by approximately one day every 126 years.
{| class=“wikitable” style=“width: 98%; border: 2px solid gray;” |- {{Ägyptologie TblHighlight}} |colspan=“6” style=“text-align:center; background:#FFE39B”|’’‘Comparison of Solar and Sothis Year (Period 2770 to 1500 BC)’’’Jean Meeus: ‘‘Astronomical Algorithms: Applications for Ephemeris Tool 4, 5.’’ Barth, Leipzig 2000 for: ‘‘Ephemeris Tool 4,5’’ according to Jean Meeus: ‘‘Conversion Program, 2001’’. |- {{Ägyptologie TblHighlight}} ! {{ill|Observation site|de|Beobachtungsort|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Observation site|qq=|WD=Q184876}} ! {{ill|1 Sothis year|de|1 Sothisjahr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1 Sothis year|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}} ! {{ill|1 solar year|de|1 Sonnenjahr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1 solar year|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}} ! {{ill|Deviation|de|Abweichung|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Deviation|qq=|WD=Q3706278}} ! {{ill|Shift of one day after:|de|Verschiebung von einem Tag nach:|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shift of one day after:|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}} |- style=“background:#F5F5F5” |[[:en:Nile Delta|Nile Delta]] |style=“text-align:center” | 365.25001 days |style=“text-align:center” | 365.24208 daysFrom [[:en:Summer solstice|summer solstice]] to the next summer solstice, based on the period 2770 BC to 1500 BC. |style=“text-align:center” | 0.0079298 days |style=“text-align:center” | 126 years + 39 days |- style=“background:#FFFAF0” |[[:en:Elephantine|Elephantine]] |style=“text-align:center” | 365.25002 days |style=“text-align:center” | 365.24208 days |style=“text-align:center” | 0.0079417 days |style=“text-align:center” | 125 years + 11 months |- |}
== See also == • [[:en:Book of Nut|Book of Nut]] • [[:en:Sothic cycle|Sothis cycle]]
== Literature == • Rolf Krauss: ‘‘Sothis and Lunar Data: Studies on the Astronomical and Technical Chronology of Ancient Egypt.’’ Gerstenberg, Hildesheim 1985, {{Falsche ISBN|3-8067-8086-X}}. • [[:en:Christian Leitz|Christian Leitz]]: ‘‘Studies on Egyptian Astronomy.’’ Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 1991, ISBN 3-447-03157-3. • {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|qq=|WD=Q2643704}}: ‘‘Outline of the Course of the Stars: The so-called Book of Nut.’’ The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (among others), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5. • {{ill|Siegfried Schott|de|Siegfried Schott|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Siegfried Schott|qq=|WD=Q102228}}: ‘‘Ancient Egyptian Festal Dates.’’ Verlag der Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur, Mainz/Wiesbaden 1950.
== References ==

== Background == With the beginning of the {{ill|Middle Kingdom|de|Mittleren Reiches|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Middle Kingdom|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}, the duration of a Sothis year—in connection with the {{ill|Coffin Texts|en|Coffin Texts|WD=Q532105|lt=Coffin Texts}} and {{ill|diagonal star clock|de|Diagonalsternuhr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/diagonal star clock|qq=|WD=Q142353}}en—was additionally applied to the periods between two {{ill|Sirius culminations|de|Sirius-Kulminationen|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sirius culminations|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}} or between two {{ill|akronychic Sirius settings|de|akronychischen Sirius-Untergängen|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/akronychic Sirius settings|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}.
Before the introduction of the Egyptian administrative calendar, to which the {{ill|Egyptian civil lunar calendar|de|ägyptische bürgerliche Mondkalender|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Egyptian civil lunar calendar|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}} was oriented, the Egyptians followed the Sothis year, which on average—at 365,250015 days—provided the original division of the Egyptian calendar year and roughly corresponded to the {{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|WD=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar}}. The {{ill|seasons|de|Jahreszeiten|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/seasons|qq=|WD=Q24384}} of the Sothis year shifted, compared to the actual {{ill|tropical year|de|Sonnenjahr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tropical year|qq=|WD=Q577}}, by approximately one day every 126 years.

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[de:Sothisjahr]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[de:Sothisjahr]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[de:Sothisjahr]] [[File:CompareTropicalYears.png|alt=Definitions for the tropical year|thumb]]
The term ‘’‘Sothis year’’’ (also ‘’’{{ill|Sopdet|en|Sopdet|WD=Q930167|lt=Sopdet}} year’’’) originally referred in {{ill|Ancient Egypt|en|Ancient Egypt|WD=Q11768|lt=Ancient Egypt}} to the {{ill|calendar period|de|Kalenderzeitraum|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/calendar period|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}} between two heliacal risings of the {{ill|Star|en|Star|WD=Q523|lt=star}} {{ill|Sirius|en|Sirius|WD=Q3409|lt=Sirius}}.
== Background == With the beginning of the {{ill|Middle Kingdom|de|Mittleren Reiches|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Middle Kingdom|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}, the duration of a Sothis year—in connection with the {{ill|Coffin Texts|en|Coffin Texts|WD=Q532105|lt=Coffin Texts}} and {{ill|diagonal star clock|de|Diagonalsternuhr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/diagonal star clock|qq=|WD=Q142353}}en—was additionally applied to the periods between two {{ill|Sirius culminations|de|Sirius-Kulminationen|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sirius culminations|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}} or between two {{ill|akronychic Sirius settings|de|akronychischen Sirius-Untergängen|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/akronychic Sirius settings|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}.
Before the introduction of the Egyptian administrative calendar, to which the {{ill|Egyptian civil lunar calendar|de|ägyptische bürgerliche Mondkalender|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Egyptian civil lunar calendar|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}} was oriented, the Egyptians followed the Sothis year, which on average—at 365,250015 days—provided the original division of the Egyptian calendar year and roughly corresponded to the {{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|WD=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar}}. The {{ill|seasons|de|Jahreszeiten|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/seasons|qq=|WD=Q24384}} of the Sothis year shifted, compared to the actual {{ill|tropical year|de|Sonnenjahr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tropical year|qq=|WD=Q577}}, by approximately one day every 126 years.
{| class=“wikitable” style=“width: 98%; border: 2px solid gray;” |- {{Ägyptologie TblHighlight}} |colspan=“6” style=“text-align:center; background:#FFE39B”|’’‘Comparison of Solar and Sothis Year (Period 2770 to 1500 BC)’’’Jean Meeus: ‘‘Astronomical Algorithms: Applications for Ephemeris Tool 4, 5.’’ Barth, Leipzig 2000 for: ‘‘Ephemeris Tool 4,5’’ according to Jean Meeus: ‘‘Conversion Program, 2001’’. |- {{Ägyptologie TblHighlight}} ! {{ill|Observation site|de|Beobachtungsort|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Observation site|qq=|WD=Q184876}} ! {{ill|1 Sothis year|de|1 Sothisjahr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1 Sothis year|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}} ! {{ill|1 solar year|de|1 Sonnenjahr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1 solar year|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}} ! {{ill|Deviation|de|Abweichung|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Deviation|qq=|WD=Q3706278}} ! {{ill|Shift of one day after:|de|Verschiebung von einem Tag nach:|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shift of one day after:|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}} |- style=“background:#F5F5F5” |{{ill|Nile Delta|en|Nile Delta|WD=Q179528|lt=Nile Delta}} |style=“text-align:center” | 365.25001 days |style=“text-align:center” | 365.24208 daysFrom {{ill|Summer solstice|en|Summer solstice|WD=Q7603898|lt=summer solstice}} to the next summer solstice, based on the period 2770 BC to 1500 BC. |style=“text-align:center” | 0.0079298 days |style=“text-align:center” | 126 years + 39 days |- style=“background:#FFFAF0” |{{ill|Elephantine|en|Elephantine|WD=Q284009|lt=Elephantine}} |style=“text-align:center” | 365.25002 days |style=“text-align:center” | 365.24208 days |style=“text-align:center” | 0.0079417 days |style=“text-align:center” | 125 years + 11 months |- |}
== See also == • {{ill|Book of Nut|en|Book of Nut|WD=Q2005559|lt=Book of Nut}} • {{ill|Sothic cycle|en|Sothic cycle|WD=Q1749234|lt=Sothis cycle}}
== Literature == • Rolf Krauss: ‘‘Sothis and Lunar Data: Studies on the Astronomical and Technical Chronology of Ancient Egypt.’’ Gerstenberg, Hildesheim 1985, {{Falsche ISBN|3-8067-8086-X}}. • {{ill|Christian Leitz|en|Christian Leitz|WD=Q1080802|lt=Christian Leitz}}: ‘‘Studies on Egyptian Astronomy.’’ Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 1991, ISBN 3-447-03157-3. • {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|qq=|WD=Q2643704}}: ‘‘Outline of the Course of the Stars: The so-called Book of Nut.’’ The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (among others), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5. • {{ill|Siegfried Schott|de|Siegfried Schott|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Siegfried Schott|qq=|WD=Q102228}}: ‘‘Ancient Egyptian Festal Dates.’’ Verlag der Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur, Mainz/Wiesbaden 1950.
== References ==

== Background == With the beginning of the {{ill|Middle Kingdom|de|Mittleren Reiches|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Middle Kingdom|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}, the duration of a Sothis year—in connection with the {{ill|Coffin Texts|en|Coffin Texts|WD=Q532105|lt=Coffin Texts|1=Coffin Texts}} and {{ill|diagonal star clock|de|Diagonalsternuhr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/diagonal star clock|qq=|WD=Q142353|1=Star clock}}en—was additionally applied to the periods between two {{ill|Sirius culminations|de|Sirius-Kulminationen|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sirius culminations|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}} or between two {{ill|akronychic Sirius settings|de|akronychischen Sirius-Untergängen|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/akronychic Sirius settings|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}.
Before the introduction of the Egyptian administrative calendar, to which the {{ill|Egyptian civil lunar calendar|de|ägyptische bürgerliche Mondkalender|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Egyptian civil lunar calendar|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}} was oriented, the Egyptians followed the Sothis year, which on average—at 365,250015 days—provided the original division of the Egyptian calendar year and roughly corresponded to the {{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|WD=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar|1=Julian calendar}}. The {{ill|seasons|de|Jahreszeiten|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/seasons|qq=|WD=Q24384|1=Season}} of the Sothis year shifted, compared to the actual {{ill|tropical year|de|Sonnenjahr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tropical year|qq=|WD=Q577|1=Year}}, by approximately one day every 126 years.
{| class=“wikitable” style=“width: 98%; border: 2px solid gray;” |- {{Ägyptologie TblHighlight}} |colspan=“6” style=“text-align:center; background:#FFE39B”|’’‘Comparison of Solar and Sothis Year (Period 2770 to 1500 BC)’’’Jean Meeus: ‘‘Astronomical Algorithms: Applications for Ephemeris Tool 4, 5.’’ Barth, Leipzig 2000 for: ‘‘Ephemeris Tool 4,5’’ according to Jean Meeus: ‘‘Conversion Program, 2001’’. |- {{Ägyptologie TblHighlight}} ! {{ill|Shift of one day after:|de|Verschiebung von einem Tag nach:|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shift of one day after:|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}} |- style=“background:#F5F5F5” |{{ill|Nile Delta|en|Nile Delta|WD=Q179528|lt=Nile Delta|1=Nile Delta}} |style=“text-align:center” | 365.25001 days |style=“text-align:center” | 365.24208 daysFrom {{ill|Summer solstice|en|Summer solstice|WD=Q7603898|lt=summer solstice|1=Summer solstice}} to the next summer solstice, based on the period 2770 BC to 1500 BC. |style=“text-align:center” | 0.0079298 days |style=“text-align:center” | 126 years + 39 days |- style=“background:#FFFAF0” |{{ill|Elephantine|en|Elephantine|WD=Q284009|lt=Elephantine|1=Elephantine}} |style=“text-align:center” | 365.25002 days |style=“text-align:center” | 365.24208 days |style=“text-align:center” | 0.0079417 days |style=“text-align:center” | 125 years + 11 months |- |}
